steps.title: Use the coincidence clue
steps.content: The sentence says two cicada broods on different cycles coincided in 2024. The blank needs a noun for separate things coming together at the same time, especially in a rare event that happens once every 221 years.

steps.title: Reject wrong event nouns
steps.content: Choice A means one thing following another, not simultaneous emergence. Choice B means splitting apart. Choice D means an ending, but the passage describes cicadas emerging. The answer is C.
